assessments.rationale: DSO is the grid operator; they allocate feeder capacity and site Netzboosters exactly where constraints exist. Constraint does not apply in the external-applicant sense.
DSOs own suitable land at existing substations; Baugenehmigung is routine for utility-owned parcels.
assessments.mitigation: Site on existing DSO substation / switching-station parcel, Package Baugenehmigung application with DSO planning department, Include Feuerwehr clearance plan in civil scope

assessments.rationale: Standard DSO fire-protection process; priced into civil scope.
assessments.mitigation: VdS 3103 compliance dossier, Fire-wall provision for >3 MWh clusters, Local Feuerwehr consultation and hydrant provisioning

assessments.rationale: Containment measures are standard for utility-scale BESS; priced into container BOM. Not absolute for market access — absolute only in the sense of chemistry.
assessments.mitigation: NOVEC 1230 / aerosol suppression per container, Deflagration venting + compartmentalization, IEC 62933-5-2 safety dossier and BSI KRITIS alignment

assessments.rationale: DSOs run this process routinely for third-party connections and know the shortcuts for their own assets.
assessments.mitigation: Parallelize FGW TR8 Type B dossier with equipment procurement, Engage FGW-accredited certifier at kickoff (TUV NORD / TUV SUD / FGH), Leverage DSO internal grid-connection department for acceptance

assessments.rationale: Utility procurement increasingly scores on EU-content and ESG; non-CN cell qualification is a competitive advantage, not a blocker.
assessments.mitigation: CRMA + EU Battery Regulation compliance dossier per cell batch, Qualify EU-assembled / non-CN cell alternatives (e.g., ACC, Verkor, Northvolt lines), Include dual-source supply clause in EPC contract

assessments.rationale: German DSO territory sits entirely within the -20 to +50 degC ambient envelope; no site exceeds it.
Netzbooster value is capacity-based (congestion relief, voltage support, substation-upgrade deferral), not energy-arbitrage throughput. 10-15 % loss does not invalidate the business case.
Cost is never a knockout. DSO recovery path is through regulated RAB, not merchant revenues — different viability math than the IPP/C&I case.
assessments.mitigation: Secure BNetzA §11 EnWG recognition of Netzbooster as alternative-to-reinforcement, Structure CAPEX for RAB rate-base inclusion under regulated revenue cap, Compare to avoided substation-reinforcement cost in business case
